data.title: Dick's Sporting Goods To Rally Around 17%? Here Are 10 Top Analyst Forecasts For Friday
data.description:
Top Wall Street analysts changed their outlook on these top names. For a complete view of all analyst rating changes, including upgrades and downgrades, please see our analyst ratings page.

  • Ascendiant Capital cut 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Inc (NASDAQ:SXTP) price target from $3 to $2.8. Ascendiant Capital analyst Lucas Ward maintained a Buy rating. 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals shares closed at $1.05 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • UBS raised the price target for Five Below Inc (NASDAQ:FIVE) from $184 to $204. UBS analyst Michael Lasser maintained a Buy rating. Five Below shares closed at $166.95 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • JP Morgan cut Cabot Corp (NYSE:CBT) price target from $75 to $54. JP Morgan analyst Jeffrey Zekauskas maintained an Underweight rating. Cabot shares closed at $63.80 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • B of A Securities lowered the price target for Bilibili Inc – ADR (NASDAQ:BILI) from $32 to $31. B of A Securities analyst Brad Sills maintained a Buy rating. Bilibili shares closed at $26.41 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • Barclays boosted the price target for Nio Inc – ADR (NYSE:NIO) from $3 to $4. Barclays analyst Jiong Shao maintained an Underweight rating. Nio shares closed at $5.46 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • Barclays slashed Dick’s Sporting Goods Inc (NYSE:DKS) price target from $246 to $242. Barclays analyst Adrienne Yih maintained an Overweight rating. Dick’s Sporting shares settled at $207.41 on Tuesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • Barclays raised Abercrombie & Fitch Co (NYSE:ANF) price target from $84 to $94. Barclays analyst Adrienne Yih maintained an Equal-Weight rating. Abercrombie & Fitch shares closed at $95.14 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • Telsey Advisory Group increased Macy’s Inc (NYSE:M) price target from $17 to $22. Telsey Advisory Group analyst Dana Telsey maintained a Market Perform rating. Macy’s shares closed at $22.43 on Wednesday.  See how other analysts view this stock.
  • B of A Securities cut the price target for Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (NYSE:BXSL) from $33.5 to $29. B of A Securities analyst Wamsi Mohan maintained a Buy rating. Blackstone Secured Lending Fund shares settled at $27.49 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.
  • JP Morgan cut Orion SA (NYSE:OEC) price target from $9 to $5. JP Morgan analyst Jeffrey Zekauskas maintained an Underweight rating. Orion shares closed at $5.15 on Wednesday. See how other analysts view this stock.

Considering buying DKS stock? Here’s what analysts think:

data.title: With Cryptocurrencies Potentially Poised For A Bounce Back, The REX Osprey XRP ETF Could Be An Alternative Worth Looking Into
data.description:
In early October, the cryptocurrency sector enjoyed an all-time record, printing a total market capitalization of $4.28 trillion. Since then, however, sentiment suffered a significant deterioration, with the valuation falling below the $3 trillion mark before witnessing the current recovery effort. Driving the red ink was a fallout in the tech ecosystem, along with anxieties tied to the Federal Reserve.

With the Trump administration setting an unprecedented course in terms of tariffs and other high-level policies, overall market sentiment was weak in the first few months of 2025. However, as investors digested the White House's leadership cadence, valuations turned skyward. Among the top performers was the tech space, led by innovators of artificial intelligence. As well, cryptocurrencies saw a blistering surge in capital inflows.

Still, amid the euphoria, an elite few tech players amassed an enormous amount of wealth. According to a new report from the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association (SIFMA), high-growth tech stocks have attracted outsized weight despite a much smaller share of corporate earnings. Basically, just 20 companies account for more than half of the S&P 500's total market value.

While stakeholders undoubtedly have enjoyed the ride up, the problem is that an unexpected sentiment regime shift can spark tremendous volatility. That's essentially what transpired with several Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) exchange-traded funds, which saw billions of dollars being drawn out in a matter of weeks. Given that BTC commands the lion's share of crypto valuations and is synonymous with the blockchain ecosystem, other alternative coins — such as XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) — stumbled in sympathy.

Adding to the downside pressure were fears focused on the Fed. Throughout the year, the Trump administration had applied relentless pressure on the central bank to cut the benchmark interest rate, with the institution finally accommodating the request in September. However, an anticipated December cut incurred low odds of materializing due to the government shutdown forcing a delay in key economic data. This meant the Fed was operating blindly.

Nevertheless, the latest data points to heightened odds that a rate cut will occur in December following dovish commentary by some Fed officials. As such, the crypto sector has been noticeably buoyant, with altcoins like XRP delivering higher returns recently compared to more established names.

The REX Shares ETF: While investing directly in digital assets like XRP may yield considerable rewards, the administrative risks are quite elevated, especially for those unaccustomed to the sector's unique nuances. From top-tier security threats down to forgetting one's password, it's all too easy for crypto riches to vanish literally in the blink of an eye.

Fortunately, financial services provider REX Shares offers an intriguing alternative known as the REX Osprey XRP ETF (BATS:XRPR). As the name implies, the XRPR is tied to the XRP virtual currency. In addition, the fund benefits from a 100% custodial structure. Effectively, XRPR's private keys that are used to store and move the underlying funds are held in digital wallets controlled by the custodian — not individual investors.

True, this structure means that investors don't directly control their holdings. However, this arrangement also provides peace of mind as the control mechanism rests somewhere outside the investors' purview, which may enhance their overall security.

But what really distinguishes the XRPR ETF from other crypto-related ETFs is the exposure profile. Fundamentally, XRPR seeks 1:1 exposure to XRP through holding the actual tokens. As such, the REX Shares fund is not a futures product nor a synthetic swap-only product. Instead, it's just about as close as an investor can get to owning XRP directly — but without the hassles associated with self-management.

Despite the advantages of the XRPR ETF, prospective participants must consider the risks. At a wider level, one of the biggest centers is regulation. While XRPR distinguishes itself as the first U.S. ETF providing direct exposure to the namesake cryptocurrency, future legal rulings could potentially kill the product. Second, from a technical perspective, virtual currencies are wildly unpredictable — altcoins being especially vulnerable to volatility. As such, extreme caution must be taken.

The XRPR ETF: A relatively new financial vehicle, the XRPR ETF launched in September of this year. So far, it is down about 29% due to the aforementioned tech sell-off.

  • While XRPR fell about 16% in the trailing month, the last five sessions have seen bullish sentiment returning, with the fund gaining roughly 5%.
  • At the moment, the 20-day exponential moving average is imposing upside resistance on XRPR's price action. However, the bulls may be attempting to break through the line.
  • One stat to watch closely is volume. So far, the level has stabilized at around 277,000 units. If the price rises, look for volume to confirm the move.

For more information on the REX-Osprey™ XRP ETF (XRPR), click here.

Featured image from Shutterstock

data.title: Bitcoin's 'Four-Year Cycle' Isn't Broken — It Was Never Real And The Data Finally Proves It
data.description:
Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) so-called four-year cycle is cracking apart, with new analysis claiming the narrative survives on bad stats and cherry-picked charts.

New Research Challenges Halving-Linked Cycle Claims

Analysts argue that the halving has always been known in advance, meaning markets continuously discount it rather than react cyclically. 

The notion that BTC resets every four years ignores how investors price information into markets daily, not at multi-year intervals.

The critique highlights that BTC's entire history contains only four such "cycles," offering far too little data to confirm a repeating pattern. 

Treating four observations as a robust statistical sample creates the illusion of a reliable framework where none exists.

Multiple Testing And Survivorship Bias Undermine Cycle Theory

A key flaw is the multiple testing problem. 

With enough backtesting across thousands of potential timeframes, some periods will appear statistically significant by chance. 

Analysts note that many cycle proponents unintentionally cherry-pick the periods that look cyclical while discarding the rest, creating a false sense of predictability.

Survivorship bias adds to the issue as popular models such as PlanB's Stock-to-Flow gained prominence when price happened to align with their forecasts, only to fail in later periods. 

As those models break, new ones emerge, giving the illusion that the cycle persists even as predictions repeatedly shift.

Non-Stationarity Limits Predictive Power In A Changing Market

Critics also point to non-stationarity — the idea that the statistical behavior of BTC changes over time. 

Liquidity, derivatives structure, institutional participation, regulatory landscape, and miner economics have all evolved dramatically since 2009. 

A pattern observed during Bitcoin's early low-liquidity era is unlikely to apply to its current market structure.

As market regimes shift, any model built on outdated parameters rapidly loses predictive power. This makes the four-year cycle particularly vulnerable to structural change.

Curve Fitting And Non-Falsifiability Erode Model Credibility

Most visual cycle charts rely on curve fitting as analysts can adjust log scales, trendline angles, smoothing functions, and starting points to make nearly any upward-drifting asset appear cyclical. 

When price deviates, the cycle is often "re-drawn" rather than abandoned, making the hypothesis non-falsifiable.

Chicago-based attorney and Bitcoin advocate Joe Carlasare summarized the sentiment in a recent X post, urging traders to "free your mind" of four-year narratives and focus instead on actual price structure and liquidity dynamics.

Bitcoin Technical Picture: Buyers Defend Key Fib Support

Bitcoin Technical Analysis (Source: TradingView)

BTC is attempting to stabilize after defending support at the $86,700 area, which aligns with the 0.382 Fibonacci retracement of the prior rally. 

Buyers have pushed price back above $91,500, although the rebound remains corrective while BTC trades below the 20-day and 50-day EMAs at $93,200 and $100,500.

The broader structure remains capped by the descending channel that has rejected every rally since the $124,000 peak. 

A break above the $93,800 to $97,000 resistance band is needed to confirm momentum rotation. 

Failure to reclaim the 0.5 and 0.618 retracement levels keeps downside targets open toward $86,700 and $81,900.

Short-term indicators show improving momentum, but traders view the move as relief until BTC closes decisively above the EMA cluster near $100,500.

  • Dogecoin Still Trapped In ‘Third-Wave’ Deadlock? Meanwhile, Popular Analyst Sees This Level As ‘Main Resistance’

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Preview: Simulations Plus's Earnings
data.description:
Simulations Plus (NASDAQ:SLP) will release its quarterly earnings report on Monday, 2025-12-01. Here's a brief overview for investors ahead of the announcement.

Analysts anticipate Simulations Plus to report an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.11.

The announcement from Simulations Plus is eagerly anticipated, with investors seeking news of surpassing estimates and favorable guidance for the next quarter.

It's worth noting for new investors that guidance can be a key determinant of stock price movements.

Performance in Previous Earnings

The company's EPS beat by $0.19 in the last quarter, leading to a 25.76% drop in the share price on the following day.

Here's a look at Simulations Plus's past performance and the resulting price change:

Market Performance of Simulations Plus's Stock

Shares of Simulations Plus were trading at $17.29 as of November 25. Over the last 52-week period, shares are down 45.58%. Given that these returns are generally negative, long-term shareholders are likely bearish going into this earnings release.

Analyst Opinions on Simulations Plus

For investors, staying informed about market sentiments and expectations in the industry is paramount. This analysis provides an exploration of the latest insights on Simulations Plus.

Simulations Plus has received a total of 1 ratings from analysts, with the consensus rating as Neutral. With an average one-year price target of $16.0, the consensus suggests a potential 7.46% downside.

Peer Ratings Comparison

The below comparison of the analyst ratings and average 1-year price targets of Sophia Genetics, Definitive Healthcare and OptimizeRx, three prominent players in the industry, gives insights for their relative performance expectations and market positioning.

  • Analysts currently favor an Buy trajectory for Sophia Genetics, with an average 1-year price target of $7.0, suggesting a potential 59.51% downside.
  • Analysts currently favor an Neutral trajectory for Definitive Healthcare, with an average 1-year price target of $4.0, suggesting a potential 76.87% downside.
  • Analysts currently favor an Outperform trajectory for OptimizeRx, with an average 1-year price target of $21.33, suggesting a potential 23.37% upside.

Comprehensive Peer Analysis Summary

In the peer analysis summary, key metrics for Sophia Genetics, Definitive Healthcare and OptimizeRx are highlighted, providing an understanding of their respective standings within the industry and offering insights into their market positions and comparative performance.

Key Takeaway:

Simulations Plus ranks at the bottom for Revenue Growth and Gross Profit, while it is in the middle for Return on Equity.

Get to Know Simulations Plus Better

Simulations Plus Inc is engaged in the software industry. It includes two segments. It develops and produces software for use in pharmaceutical research and education, and provides consulting and contract research services to the pharmaceutical industry. The company's operating segments include Software and services. The company offers software products for pharmaceutical research such as ADMET (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion, and Toxicity). It generates maximum revenue from the software segment. Maximum revenue is earned from USA following EMEA and Asia Pacific.

Understanding the Numbers: Simulations Plus's Finances

Market Capitalization Analysis: Reflecting a smaller scale, the company's market capitalization is positioned below industry averages. This could be attributed to factors such as growth expectations or operational capacity.

Revenue Growth: Simulations Plus's remarkable performance in 3 months is evident. As of 31 May, 2025, the company achieved an impressive revenue growth rate of 9.81%. This signifies a substantial increase in the company's top-line earnings. In comparison to its industry peers, the company trails behind with a growth rate lower than the average among peers in the Health Care sector.

Net Margin: The company's net margin is below industry benchmarks, signaling potential difficulties in achieving strong profitability. With a net margin of -330.58%, the company may need to address challenges in effective cost control.

Return on Equity (ROE): Simulations Plus's ROE is below industry averages, indicating potential challenges in efficiently utilizing equity capital. With an ROE of -42.97%, the company may face hurdles in achieving optimal financial returns.

Return on Assets (ROA): Simulations Plus's ROA is below industry standards, pointing towards difficulties in efficiently utilizing assets. With an ROA of -40.1%, the company may encounter challenges in delivering satisfactory returns from its assets.

Debt Management: The company maintains a balanced debt approach with a debt-to-equity ratio below industry norms, standing at 0.01.
